name: Atomic Operations & CAS
def: Indivisible read-modify-write operations (compare-and-swap, fetch-add) the hardware guarantees atomic.
problem: Coordinate threads without locks, building counters, flags and lock-free structures.
mechanism: CPU instructions (LOCK CMPXCHG, LL/SC) perform the op atomically; memory-ordering parameters control visibility relative to other operations.
limits: Memory ordering is hard to reason about
whenNot: Complex multi-word invariants (use a lock or STM)
myths: "Atomic = thread-safe algorithm" — atomicity of one op does not make a multi-step sequence safe
